edges.description: AT-1 deficiency limits lumenal acetyl-CoA supply for normal protein and ganglioside acetylation.
Secretory-pathway dysfunction disrupts normal brain development.
Secretory-pathway dysfunction also impairs development of the hearing system.
Secretory-pathway dysfunction disrupts normal lens development.
AT-1 loss reduces secretion of ceruloplasmin from hepatocyte-like cells.
Reduced ceruloplasmin secretion lowers circulating ceruloplasmin.
Reduced ceruloplasmin secretion lowers circulating ceruloplasmin.
Lower circulating ceruloplasmin decreases measured serum copper.
Lower circulating ceruloplasmin decreases measured serum copper.
Disrupted brain development contributes to intellectual disability.
Interrupted brain development produces severe developmental delay.
Impaired cerebellar development is expressed as cerebellar hypoplasia.
Abnormal brain development is expressed radiographically as CNS hypomyelination.
Cerebellar and broader CNS injury is associated with spastic ataxia in the adult phenotype.
Brain-development injury is associated with hypotonia in the adult phenotype.
Brain-development injury is associated with tremor in the adult phenotype.
Cochlear developmental injury is expressed clinically as sensorineural hearing impairment.
Lens developmental injury is expressed clinically as congenital cataract.
Low serum ceruloplasmin reports reduced ceruloplasmin secretion downstream of AT-1 deficiency.
Low serum copper reflects reduced ceruloplasmin-bound copper rather than primary copper deficiency.

nodes.description: Central nervous system hypomyelination is a characteristic MRI finding.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Cerebral MRI showed pronounced cerebellar hypoplasia and hypomyelination.

nodes.description: Congenital cataracts are one of the signature presenting features of Huppke-Brendel syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: We report on five patients from four unrelated families with these biochemical findings who presented with a lethal autosomal-recessive syndrome of congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and severe developmental delay.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Cerebral MRI showed pronounced cerebellar hypoplasia and hypomyelination.

nodes.description: Defective AT-1-dependent acetylation disrupts normal brain development and contributes to cerebellar hypoplasia and hypomyelination.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: The severity of the phenotype implies an essential role of AT-1 in proper posttranslational modification of numerous proteins, without which normal lens and brain development is interrupted.

nodes.description: AT-1 deficiency disrupts normal auditory-system development and contributes to congenital sensorineural hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: We report on five patients from four unrelated families with these biochemical findings who presented with a lethal autosomal-recessive syndrome of congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and severe developmental delay.

nodes.description: Reduced circulating ceruloplasmin is a defining biochemical feature of the syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, AT-1 defects are a new and important differential diagnosis in patients with low copper and ceruloplasmin in serum.

nodes.description: Reduced circulating copper is a defining biochemical feature of the syndrome and is secondary to low ceruloplasmin.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, AT-1 defects are a new and important differential diagnosis in patients with low copper and ceruloplasmin in serum.

nodes.description: Loss of AT-1 function disrupts acetylation-dependent processing of glycoproteins and gangliosides in the secretory pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Deep sequencing and conventional sequencing disclosed homozygous or compound heterozygous mutations for all affected subjects in SLC33A1 encoding a highly conserved acetylCoA transporter (AT-1) required for acetylation of multiple gangliosides and glycoproteins.

nodes.description: Hypotonia has been documented in the adult phenotypic spectrum of Huppke-Brendel syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Case report: Huppke-Brendel syndrome in an adult, mistaken for and treated as Wilson disease for 25 years.
evidence.snippet: The patient suffered from moderate intellectual disability, partial hearing loss, spastic ataxia, hypotonia, and unilateral tremor of parkinsonian type.

nodes.description: Severe developmental delay or later intellectual disability is a prominent neurologic manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Case report: Huppke-Brendel syndrome in an adult, mistaken for and treated as Wilson disease for 25 years.
evidence.snippet: The patient suffered from moderate intellectual disability, partial hearing loss, spastic ataxia, hypotonia, and unilateral tremor of parkinsonian type.

nodes.description: AT-1 deficiency disrupts normal lens development and produces congenital cataract.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: The severity of the phenotype implies an essential role of AT-1 in proper posttranslational modification of numerous proteins, without which normal lens and brain development is interrupted.

nodes.description: AT-1 deficiency reduces ceruloplasmin secretion, explaining the low serum ceruloplasmin and secondary low serum copper pattern without primary copper deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: We also showed that AT-1 knockdown in HepG2 cells leads to reduced ceruloplasmin secretion, indicating that the low copper in serum is due to reduced ceruloplasmin levels and is not a sign of copper deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Deep sequencing and conventional sequencing disclosed homozygous or compound heterozygous mutations for all affected subjects in SLC33A1 encoding a highly conserved acetylCoA transporter (AT-1) required for acetylation of multiple gangliosides and glycoproteins.

nodes.description: Biallelic SLC33A1 variants impair the endoplasmic-reticulum acetyl-CoA transporter AT-1.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Deep sequencing and conventional sequencing disclosed homozygous or compound heterozygous mutations for all affected subjects in SLC33A1 encoding a highly conserved acetylCoA transporter (AT-1) required for acetylation of multiple gangliosides and glycoproteins.

nodes.description: Hearing loss is part of the classic syndromic triad.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: We report on five patients from four unrelated families with these biochemical findings who presented with a lethal autosomal-recessive syndrome of congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and severe developmental delay.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, AT-1 defects are a new and important differential diagnosis in patients with low copper and ceruloplasmin in serum.

readouts.interpretation: Low serum ceruloplasmin reports reduced ceruloplasmin secretion downstream of AT-1 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, AT-1 defects are a new and important differential diagnosis in patients with low copper and ceruloplasmin in serum.

readouts.interpretation: Low serum copper reflects reduced ceruloplasmin-bound copper rather than primary copper deficiency.

nodes.description: Severe developmental delay is part of the classic lethal infantile presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in SLC33A1 cause a lethal autosomal-recessive disorder with congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and low serum copper and ceruloplasmin.
evidence.snippet: We report on five patients from four unrelated families with these biochemical findings who presented with a lethal autosomal-recessive syndrome of congenital cataracts, hearing loss, and severe developmental delay.

nodes.description: Spastic ataxia has been reported in an adult patient with Huppke-Brendel syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Case report: Huppke-Brendel syndrome in an adult, mistaken for and treated as Wilson disease for 25 years.
evidence.snippet: The patient suffered from moderate intellectual disability, partial hearing loss, spastic ataxia, hypotonia, and unilateral tremor of parkinsonian type.

nodes.description: Parkinsonian-type tremor has been reported in an adult patient with Huppke-Brendel syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Case report: Huppke-Brendel syndrome in an adult, mistaken for and treated as Wilson disease for 25 years.
evidence.snippet: The patient suffered from moderate intellectual disability, partial hearing loss, spastic ataxia, hypotonia, and unilateral tremor of parkinsonian type.
